topic: Best Ollama Models for Coding 2026: Ranked and Tested

competitors.title: Best Ollama Models for Coding in 2026 — We Tested 10 Models, Here's the Ranking
key_points: Tests 10 models on real hardware (RTX 4090, RTX 3090, MacBook Pro M3 Max)
Covers benchmark scores, token throughput, and VRAM consumption
Qwen 3.6-27B named top pick with 77.2% SWE-bench Verified
Devstral Small 24B highlighted for agentic coding workflows
Includes hardware tier breakdowns and practical recommendations

competitors.title: Best Ollama Models: 12 Models Ranked for Coding, RAG & Agents (2026)
key_points: Covers 12 models ranked for coding, RAG, and agent use cases
Includes Qwen2.5-Coder 32B (92.7% HumanEval), Devstral Small 2 (68% SWE-bench Verified)
Details VRAM tiers: 8GB, 16GB, 24GB, 40GB+
Recommends Codestral 22B for FIM/autocomplete use case
Includes quantization guide (Q4_K_M recommended for most users)

competitors.title: Best Local AI for Coding 2026: 10 Models Tested & Ranked
key_points: Focuses on practical offline coding use cases
DeepSeek R1 14B highlighted for chain-of-thought debugging
Qwen 2.5-Coder 7B recommended for budget/small hardware (72% HumanEval + FIM support)
Covers agentic scenarios: multi-file edits, terminal automation
Provides setup instructions for Ollama integration

competitors.title: Best Local Coding Models Ranked: Every VRAM Tier, Every Benchmark (2026)
key_points: Organizes models by VRAM tier: 6GB, 8GB, 12GB, 16GB, 24GB, 40GB+
Covers HumanEval, MBPP, SWE-bench across all tiers
Performance gap: models fitting in VRAM run 11x faster vs CPU offloading
GPT-OSS 20B achieves 139.93 tokens/sec vs 12.64 for 120B with offloading
Includes quantization format comparison: Q4_K_M vs Q5_K_M vs Q8

competitors.title: Best Ollama Models for Coding in 2026
key_points: Covers top models: Llama 3.3, Qwen2.5-Coder, Mistral, Devstral, DeepSeek
Llama 3.1 leads Ollama with 111 million total downloads
DeepSeek-R1 at 79 million downloads, fastest growing reasoning model
Practical command-line setup guide included
Discusses use cases: code generation, completion, debugging, documentation

stats.fact: Ollama hit 52 million monthly downloads in Q1 2026 — a 520x increase from 100,000 in Q1 2023
Ollama GitHub repository has accumulated 169,000+ stars
135,000 GGUF models available on HuggingFace for local inference via Ollama
Qwen2.5-Coder 32B achieves 92.7% on HumanEval — surpassing GPT-4's 87.1%
Devstral Small 2 (24B) scores 68% on SWE-bench Verified and runs on a single RTX 4090 or 32GB Mac
Qwen 3.6-27B scores 77.2% on SWE-bench Verified, needs just 22GB VRAM, Apache 2.0 license
Devstral achieves 46.8% on SWE-Bench Verified, outperforming prior open-source SOTA by 6%
Llama 3.1 leads Ollama with 111 million downloads; DeepSeek-R1 at 79 million
Cloud API costs per developer: $50–200/month; team of 10 = $6,000–$24,000/year
stats.source: pooya.blog / sitepoint.com Local vs Cloud AI Coding 2026

stats.fact: Local models now handle 70–80% of everyday coding tasks at zero inference cost
Models fitting fully in VRAM run ~11x faster than models requiring CPU offloading
Qwen2.5-Coder 7B scores 72% HumanEval with FIM (fill-in-the-middle) support

angles: Hardware tier guide: which Ollama coding model fits your exact GPU (6GB / 8GB / 12GB / 16GB / 24GB / 40GB+)
Agentic coding vs autocomplete: different use cases need different models — Devstral for agents, Codestral for FIM
The price-performance inflection: when paying $100–300/month for API access vs buying a one-time GPU pays off
Benchmark reality check: HumanEval vs SWE-bench Verified — why the two scores tell very different stories
The 11x speed cliff: why fitting your model in VRAM is the single biggest performance decision
Privacy-first development: Ollama 52M downloads shows local AI is now enterprise mainstream, not hobbyist niche
